message:
[P2] fcp-mesh/node: close zone-authorization bypass on unknown peers and empty zone state

Closes flywheel_connectors-opoux.

Pre-fix, `verify_summary_signature` and `verify_revocation_push_signature`
(node.rs:1339-1347 / 1383-1391) wrapped the zone gate in

    if let Some(state) = self.peers.get(&peer) {
        if !state.zones.is_empty() && !state.zones.contains(&summary.zone_id) {
            return Err(MeshNodeError::UnauthorizedZone { .. });
        }
    }

Both the `if let Some` and the `!state.zones.is_empty()` guards were
silent fallthroughs to `Ok(peer)`. `register_peer_signing_key` and
`update_peer_state` / `update_peer_zones` are independent
insertions: a peer with a valid signing key but no completed
enrollment had an entry in `peer_signing_keys` and no entry in
`peers`, so every signed message from that peer skipped the zone
check entirely. An attacker authorized only for `z:public` could
ship gossip/revocation messages tagged `z:private` and have them
accepted.

Fix: both verifiers now require `self.peers.get(&peer)` to be
populated (new `MeshNodeError::UnknownPeer { peer, message_kind }`
for the missing case) AND require `state.zones.contains(&zone_id)`
with no empty-set fallthrough. An authenticated peer whose zone
set is empty is treated as "authorized for nothing" — matches the
planner's existing conservative treatment of empty-zone candidates
at planner.rs `zone_policy_rejects_nodes_with_unknown_zones`.

The blocker bead (`flywheel_connectors-fqzmp`, planner zones never
populated) is already closed (commit a75b9e9d), so zone state is
now actually wired through `update_local_zones` /
`update_peer_zones`. This makes the strict refusal safe —
pre-fqzmp the empty-zone gate would have rejected every peer
because state was never populated.

Tests updated:

* `handle_summary_requires_valid_signature` /
  `handle_revocation_push_returns_verified_descriptor` /
  `handle_revocation_push_rejects_stale_message` — each now calls
  `update_peer_state` + `update_peer_zones` so the happy paths
  reflect the realistic bootstrap order (key registered +
  enrollment completed + summary accepted).

* New `handle_summary_rejects_unknown_peer_without_peer_state` —
  documents the primary opoux attack shape. A peer with a valid
  signing key but no entry in `self.peers` ships a
  correctly-signed summary claiming `ZoneId::work()`; pre-fix the
  summary was accepted and `gossip_updates` incremented; now
  `MeshNodeError::UnknownPeer { message_kind: "gossip summary" }`
  is returned and no state is mutated.

* New `handle_revocation_push_rejects_unknown_peer_without_peer_state`
  — symmetric coverage for the revocation-push verifier.

* New `handle_summary_rejects_peer_with_empty_zone_state` — covers
  the second bypass: enrolled peer with `update_peer_zones` never
  called. Previously `state.zones.is_empty()` bypassed; now the
  peer gets `MeshNodeError::UnauthorizedZone`.

* New `handle_summary_rejects_peer_claiming_unauthorized_zone` —
  positive-coverage test for the already-working branch (peer
  enrolled for `ZoneId::work()` ships a summary tagged
  `ZoneId::private()` and is rejected).

Verification:
  CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/fcp-p7-t11 cargo test -p fcp-mesh
    → 1181 passed; 2 failed (coordinator fencing-token tests,
      pre-existing and out of scope — same failures noted in kzrfp
      commit dfe0354a, outside this pane's domain).

fcp-host lib+tests fail to compile in the same target dir due to
unrelated symbol breakage from another pane's recent changes
(`RevocationFreshnessCheck` unresolved at multiple sites,
`ObjectHeader::metadata` field missing, `EnforcementContextBuilder::token_id`
method missing, `RevocationObject::schema` missing). None of those
live inside this fcp-mesh patch; the diff only touches
`crates/fcp-mesh/src/node.rs`. Full `cargo test -p fcp-mesh -p fcp-host`
is blocked on those external compile errors — see bead comment.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
